input.input: Search the Hugging Face Hub for three recent diffusion-model papers or official model cards that are practically useful for ML engineers. Prefer items released in 2025–2026; if insufficient, choose the most recent high-quality entries (2024–2026). For each selected item provide: title, link (hub URL), and one-line explanation of why it is relevant to ML engineers (practical use, features, speed, etc.). Then write a short comparative summary (approx 150–300 words) focusing specifically on: quality (sample fidelity), inference speed, compute requirements (GPU memory / recommended hardware), and best use case for each model. Finally, save a concise digest (about 300-500 words total) that includes the three-item shortlist with the one-line reasons and the comparative summary to these two files in the repository: /_outputs/diffusion_models_summary.md and /_outputs/hf_task_output/diffusion_models_summary.md. The digest should be written in Markdown. Use as few files as practical (just those two identical files). Return only: the paths written and a short (1-2 sentence) summary of what you saved. Do not include full file contents in your response. If any of the items require specific model repo names or tags to identify, include them in the digest. Prefer official model cards or well-documented repos. Use the Hub (not external web) as the primary source.

output:
/_outputs/diffusion_models_summary.md
/_outputs/hf_task_output/diffusion_models_summary.md

Saved a Markdown digest listing three recent StabilityAI diffusion model cards (stable-diffusion-3.5-large, stable-diffusion-3.5-large-tensorrt, stable-diffusion-3.5-large-turbo_amdgpu) with one-line reasons and a concise comparative summary covering quality, inference speed, compute guidance, and best use cases.
